label: Lincoln CSG transits Strait of Hormuz
IRGC naval exercise Strait of Hormuz
Israel intel brief: Iran missile rebuild
US campaign plan: nuclear + state targets
Israel → IRGC Air Force HQ (Tehran)
notes: Destroyed IRGC Air Force HQ, Space & Satellite Command, and 50 ammo bunkers

label: Israel → Kish Island Airport
notes: Fuel depot at Kish Island airport destroyed. Iranian naval ship Bandar Lengeh also destroyed in Bandar Abbas.

label: Israel → Ghobeiry, Beirut (Quds Force)
notes: Strike on hotel targeting meeting of five IRGC Quds Force commanders. Major explosion reported.

label: Iran → Bahrain (Bapco Refinery)
damage: Bapco refinery struck, force majeure declared
notes: Strikes on Sitra island south of Manama. Bapco Energies declared force majeure on refinery operations.

label: Iran → Fujairah Oil Facility
damage: Fire at Fujairah Oil Tanks area from debris of intercepted missiles
notes: UAE air defenses intercepted most projectiles; debris caused fire at oil facility.

label: Iran → Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
damage: Compound hit in Al-Kharj
notes: IRGC said strike targeted radar systems. Saudi Arabia intercepted UAVs heading toward Shaybah oil field.

label: Iran → Qatar
notes: Qatar's Ministry of Defence announced 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones fired from Iran; all intercepted. Air raid alerts issued ~03:15 local time.

label: Iran → Israel
notes: First wave of missiles under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. NATO also intercepted Iranian missile headed for Turkey.

label: Israel → Tehran Aghdasieh Fuel Depot
damage: Aghdasieh oil warehouse destroyed; toxic smoke cloud over Tehran
casualties: 40 killed across Tehran fuel depot strikes
notes: Part of coordinated Israeli strikes on 4 Tehran-area fuel depots. Thick black toxic smoke hung over city of 10 million.

label: Israel → Tehran Shahran Oil Depot
damage: Shahran oil depot north of Tehran destroyed
notes: Second target in coordinated fuel depot strikes. Oil-tainted rainfall reported staining buildings.

label: Israel → Karaj Fuel Depot
damage: Karaj fuel depot destroyed
notes: Third target in coordinated strikes. Israeli military stated fuel complexes distributed fuel to multiple military entities.

label: Iran → Yehud, Israel (Cluster Warhead)
damage: Multiple sites struck across central Israel
casualties: 2 civilians killed, dozens wounded in Yehud
notes: Cluster warhead munitions used in populated areas. At least 14 civilians killed in Israel since conflict began.

label: Ford CSG → Iran (Multi-Province Strikes)
notes: Continued strikes as US forces approach 2,000 total targets. Both US and Israel claim to control Iranian airspace.

label: B-2 (Diego Garcia) → Shiraz Military Infrastructure
notes: Strikes on nuclear facilities, military infrastructure, and oil refineries across multiple provinces.

label: Ford CSG → Iran ('Most Intense Day')
notes: Hegseth vowed 'most intense day of strikes.' 5,000+ total targets across 30 of 31 provinces. 60% missile launchers neutralized, air defenses degraded 80%.

label: B-2 (Diego Garcia) → Central Iran
notes: Strikes as part of most intense day. 43 Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged. Iran ballistic missile rate collapsed 92%.

label: Lincoln CSG → Strait of Hormuz Mine-Laying Vessels
damage: 16 mine-laying vessels destroyed
notes: Iran began laying mines in Strait of Hormuz. Trump ultimatum: 'death, fire and fury.' CENTCOM hunting mine-laying vessels and storage facilities.

label: Iran → Jordan (119 Missiles/Drones)
notes: Jordan announced 119 Iranian missiles and drones targeted its territory.

label: Iran → Israel (Continued Barrages)
notes: Iran ballistic missile launch rate collapsed 92% from 480/day to ~40/day. Drone launches also fell 92%.

label: Iran → UAE (Continued Bombardment)
notes: UAE cumulative: 1,468 projectiles absorbed. Iran distributing fire across 13 countries to maximize regional economic disruption.

label: Iran → Kuwait
notes: Kuwait cumulative: 562 projectiles. Part of Iran's strategy to distribute fire across 13 countries.

label: IRGC → Persian Gulf Shipping (6 vessels)
damage: 6 vessels struck over 2 days (3 on Mar 11, 3 on Mar 12) including US-owned tanker off Iraq. 500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Oman fuel depot hit.
casualties: Multiple crew injuries across struck vessels
notes: Iran claimed responsibility for striking US-owned tanker. Two more tankers struck near Iraq's southern ports — first such strikes in Iraqi waters. IRGC intensifying attacks on commercial shipping to enforce Hormuz closure.

label: Hezbollah → Northern Israel (Largest Barrage)
damage: Heaviest Hezbollah rocket attack against Israel since conflict began. Heavy volleys toward northern Israel overnight. Residents repeatedly driven into shelters.
notes: IRGC confirmed joint attack with Hezbollah on Israel — 50+ targets across 5 hours on March 12. Israel responded with airstrikes against Hezbollah launch sites and command infrastructure.

label: IRGC+Hezbollah Joint → Israel (50+ targets)
damage: IRGC confirmed joint operation with Hezbollah striking 50+ Israeli targets across 5-hour sustained barrage. Most intensive coordinated Iran-Hezbollah attack on Israel to date.
notes: Israeli DM Katz warned Lebanon: if Hezbollah cannot be controlled Israel will 'take the territory.' Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam: Lebanon 'did not want' this war.

label: IDF → Iranian Airfields (6 Bases)
notes: IDF struck 6 military airfields, destroyed 16 Quds Force transport aircraft, combat helicopters, and cratered runways

label: IDF → IRGC Space & Satellite Command
notes: Command center for Iran's ballistic missile operations destroyed alongside 50 ammo bunkers

label: IDF → Ahvaz Drone Command HQ
notes: IRGC Aerospace Force Ali Akbar Drone Base in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province cratered

label: IDF → Bandar Abbas (Ship Bandar Lengeh)
notes: Iranian navy ship Bandar Lengeh destroyed in port of Bandar Abbas

label: IDF → Isfahan (IRGC Commanders)
notes: Eliminated Abolghasem Babaeian (Supreme Leader military office chief) and Haj Ali Hashem (senior IRGC official)

label: Iran → Shaybah Oil Field (Intercepted)
notes: Saudi Arabia intercepted 4 Iranian UAVs heading for the Shaybah oil field

label: Iran → Turkey (NATO Intercept, Gaziantep)
notes: Second Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO integrated air defense over Sahinbey district, Gaziantep

label: Iran → Northern Israel (7 Waves)
notes: 12 attack waves on Mar 9; 7 targeted northern region (58%), 4 central (33%), 1 south (8%)

label: Iran → Haifa Oil Refineries
notes: IRGC targeted Haifa oil/gas refineries in retaliation for Israeli strikes on Tehran fuel depots. Tasnim confirmed.

label: Iran → Tel Aviv (Overnight Cluster Barrage)
notes: Several overnight missile barrages with cluster warheads targeting civilian areas south and east of Tel Aviv

label: Israel → Tehran South Refinery
notes: Targeted Tehran refinery in south of city; 40 killed total across 4 fuel depot strikes; toxic smoke over city of 10M

label: Iran → Haifa ('Most Intense Operation')
notes: IRGC 'most intense operation since start of war' — fired advanced Kheibar Shekan and Haj Qassem missiles at Tel Aviv and Haifa

label: Iran → Bahrain (Continued)
notes: Continued Iranian strikes on Bahrain. Cumulative: 281 projectiles absorbed by Bahrain since Feb 28

label: Iran → Saudi Arabia (Continued)
notes: Saudi air defenses intercepting Iranian drones and missiles. 2 civilians previously killed in Al-Kharj.

label: IRGC Mine-Laying — Strait of Hormuz
notes: Iran confirmed laying mines in Strait of Hormuz. Few dozen mines placed. Ship transits down 95% since Mar 1 (55 vs 160 prior week)

label: IRGC → Commercial Ship (Strait of Hormuz)
notes: Cargo ship struck in Strait of Hormuz. Oman navy rescued 20 sailors from Thai-flagged vessel.

label: Israel → Beirut (4 Iranian Diplomats Killed)
notes: Israeli airstrike in Beirut killed 4 Iranian diplomats. Iran demanded justice at UNSC, called it 'terrorist attack'.

label: Israel → Lebanon (Hezbollah Launch Sites)
notes: Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah launch sites and command infrastructure in response to IRGC+Hezbollah joint 50-target attack

label: Iran → Oman (Salalah Fuel Depot)
notes: Iranian drones struck oil storage at Port of Salalah, Oman. Emergency crews battled fire at fuel tanks.

label: Iran → Bahrain (Muharraq Fuel Tanks)
notes: Iranian strike on fuel tanks in Muharraq Governorate. Residents told to stay inside and close windows.

label: Iran → Jerusalem (Near Old City)
notes: Missile struck few hundred meters from Old City, Western Wall, Al-Aqsa, Church of Holy Sepulchre. Prayer suspended at all holy sites.

label: IRGC → Container Ship (Off UAE Coast)
notes: Container ship struck off UAE coast. Part of 6 vessels hit in 48 hours. ~500 tankers stranded.

label: IRGC → US-Owned Tanker (Iraq Waters)
notes: First strike in Iraqi waters since war began. Iran claimed responsibility for striking US-owned tanker.

label: Iran → UAE (Continued Strikes)
notes: UAE has absorbed 1,468 cumulative projectiles since Feb 28 — highest of any target country. Minor drone incidents in Dubai.

label: Iran → Saudi Arabia (Continued)
notes: Saudi Arabia shot down drones headed towards oilfield. Continued defensive operations.

label: IRGC → 3 Ships (Persian Gulf Overnight)
notes: 3 more foreign ships struck in Persian Gulf overnight. UKMTO reports 16 attacks on shipping since Feb 28. Only 5 tankers departed in 24hrs.

label: Iran → Israel (Day 14 Barrages)
notes: Continued Iranian barrages on Israel. Missile rate down 92% from peak but still ~40/day. 300+ missiles total at Israel, half with cluster warheads.

label: Iran → Gulf States (Day 14)
notes: Continued bombardment of UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia. IRGC warned 'get ready for oil at $200/bbl'.

label: US-Israel → Iran (6,000+ Targets Total)
notes: CENTCOM struck 6,000+ targets across 30/31 provinces. 90+ Iranian vessels destroyed. 60% missile launchers neutralized. 80% air defense degraded.

label: Lincoln CSG → Hormuz Mine Clearance
notes: Continued mine clearance operations. 30+ mine-laying ships destroyed total. 90+ Iranian naval vessels damaged/destroyed overall.

label: Iran Drone → Iraq Kurdistan (French Base)
notes: Drone strike on Mala Qara base in Iraqi Kurdistan. 1 French soldier killed, 6 injured. First French combat fatality.

label: KC-135 Crash — Western Iraq
notes: Two KC-135 tankers supporting Iran ops involved in incident over Iraq. One crashed in western Iraq (6 crew missing), other landed safely in Israel. First KC-135 loss in 13 years. Not due to hostile fire per CENTCOM.

label: Hezbollah → Northern Israel (Continued)
notes: Continued Hezbollah rocket salvos at northern Israel. Lebanon death toll: 634+, 800K displaced.

label: Iran → Palmachim / Ovda Airbases (Most Intense Barrage)
notes: Iran's 'most intense operation since beginning of war.' Targets: Palmachim Airbase, Ovda Airbase, Shin Bet HQ. 58 Israelis hospitalized. Most advanced ballistic missiles deployed.

label: Iran → Shin Bet HQ, Tel Aviv
notes: IRGC claimed direct strike on Shin Bet (internal security) headquarters in central Israel as part of most intense barrage of the war.

label: Iran Underwater Drones → Basra (Safesea Vishnu + Zefyros)
notes: Iran launched underwater drone 'suicide boats' against US-owned MV Safesea Vishnu and MV Zefyros in Iraqi waters near Basra. First oil-related strike in Iraq's territorial waters. 1 Indian crew member killed, 25 total crew rescued. Iraq oil terminals suspended operations.
damage: Both tankers set ablaze; Iraq oil terminals suspended

label: Iran Drones → Kuwait International Airport
notes: Multiple drones targeted Kuwait International Airport, causing material damage to runway infrastructure. 6 electricity transmission lines went out after intercepted drone debris fell on power infrastructure. Earlier a drone struck a residential building wounding 2.
damage: Material damage to airport; 6 electricity lines disrupted

label: Iran Drone → Salalah Port (Oman Fuel Tanks)
notes: Drone attack damaged several fuel storage tanks at Port of Salalah, Oman. Condemned by Qatar as 'dangerous escalation.' Oman's navy separately rescued 20 sailors from a Thai-flagged vessel attacked in the Strait of Hormuz.
damage: Fuel tanks at Salalah port set ablaze

label: IDF → Shiraz (Missile Production + Dastghaib AB)
notes: IDF struck underground ballistic missile production and storage facilities in Shiraz and Shahid Dastghaib air base. Targets Iran's remaining missile production capacity in southern Iran.

label: IDF → Ahvaz (IRGC Ground Forces HQ)
notes: Israeli strike on IRGC Ground Forces HQ and internal security HQ in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province. Region borders Iraq and hosts Iran's main oil production.

label: US → Kharg Island (Iran's Main Oil Export Terminal)
notes: US bombed Kharg Island — Iran's primary oil export terminal handling ~90% of crude exports. Pentagon vowed to 'ramp up' campaign. Most economically significant infrastructure strike of the war. Analysts warn $200/bbl possible.
damage: Kharg Island oil loading infrastructure struck

label: Iran → UAE (9 ballistic + 33 UAV)
notes: UAE intercepted 9 ballistic missiles and 33 UAVs in coordinated barrage — all intercepted per UAE Ministry of Defense.

label: Iran → Saudi Riyadh (7 drones)
notes: Saudi Arabia intercepted 7 Iranian drones targeting Riyadh per Saudi Civil Defense.

label: Iran → Qatar (4 ballistic)
notes: Qatar intercepted 4 ballistic missiles per Qatar Ministry of Interior. Al Udeid Air Base area targeted.

label: Iran → Baghdad US Embassy
damage: US Embassy helipad destroyed, air defense system destroyed
notes: Missile struck US Embassy Baghdad helipad, destroying the facility's air defense system. No US casualties reported.

label: Iran 6th Wave → Tel Nof AB (IRGC)
notes: IRGC 6th declared wave of operations. Targeted Tel Nof Airbase (IAF F-35I squadron home), Israeli Military GHQ, and Tel Aviv defense industry complex. Sirens across central Israel. Cluster fragments struck Tel Aviv streets.

label: Iran 6th Wave → Tel Aviv Military HQ + Defense Complex
notes: Part of IRGC 6th wave. Targeted Israeli Military General Headquarters (Kirya) and a defense industry complex in central Tel Aviv area. Iron Dome and David's Sling activated.

label: US-Israel → Iran (Day 17 Continued Ops)
notes: Continuation of high-tempo operations. IDF states thousands of targets remain. CENTCOM 6,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28. IDF plans 3+ more weeks of operations.

label: Iran → Dubai Int'l Airport (Drone Strike)
damage: Fuel tank ignited; large fire; Emirates suspended all flights
notes: Shahed-type drone struck fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport. Emirates airline temporarily suspended all flights. Fire brought under control after several hours.

label: Iran → Abu Dhabi (Missile Strike)
damage: Vehicle destroyed in Abu Dhabi street
notes: Ballistic missile hit a car in Abu Dhabi, killing a Palestinian resident. No other casualties reported.

label: Iran → Fujairah Industrial Zone (Drone)
damage: Industrial zone structures damaged
notes: Drone strike on Fujairah industrial zone. No reported casualties. UAE emergency services responded.

label: Iran → Kuwait City US Embassy (Missile)
damage: Embassy compound structural damage
casualties: Several Kuwaiti security personnel injured; no US fatalities
notes: Ballistic missile struck US Embassy Kuwait City compound. Secretary Rubio ordered embassy closed and staff evacuated to Bahrain. Second direct strike on a US diplomatic facility during the conflict.

label: Iran → Saudi Eastern Province (37 Drones)
notes: Saudi Arabia intercepted all 37 Iranian drones over the Eastern Province. No casualties or infrastructure damage. Aramco and Saudi Electricity Company infrastructure remain on high alert.

label: IDF → Tehran (Day 18 Strikes)
damage: Infrastructure targets; Iranian Red Crescent clinic damaged
notes: Continued high-tempo strikes on Tehran. Black smoke reported over multiple districts. Iranian Red Crescent clinic and aid relief post damaged. ICRC condemns potential humanitarian law violation. IDF F-35I shot down Iranian Yak-130 fighter jet over Tehran — first stealth-vs-manned aerial kill in history.

label: IDF → IRGC Security Unit HQ (Day 19)
damage: IRGC Security Unit HQ struck; IRGC General Logistics/Maintenance Center hit; ballistic missile array command center destroyed
notes: Day 19 IDF strikes targeting IRGC Security Unit headquarters, IRGC General Logistics and Support Department maintenance center, and a ballistic missile array command center. Netanyahu: 'The IDF is watching you from the skies of Iran.' IDF authorized to eliminate senior Iranian/Hezbollah figures without prior political approval.

label: US → Hormuz-Area Missile Sites (Day 19)
platform: USS Abraham Lincoln CSG / B-52 / B-2
damage: Hardened Iranian missile sites near Strait of Hormuz struck
notes: US forces deployed 5,000-pound GBU-28/57-type deep-penetrating bunker buster munitions against hardened Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz. Intended to reduce IRGC capacity to threaten commercial shipping. Hormuz traffic subsequently nearly doubled — Iran may have allowed more vessels through as tactical concession.

label: Iran → Ramat Gan (Cluster Missile)
damage: Elderly couple (70s) killed — first confirmed Israeli cluster munition fatalities. 8 shrapnel sites across greater Tel Aviv. Savidor Central Station damaged.
notes: IRGC explicitly stated the cluster missile barrage was 'in revenge for the killing of Larijani' — confirming Ali Larijani's assassination. Cluster submunitions scattered across urban Ramat Gan. Israeli medics described a 'new battlefield' of unexploded cluster munitions. Iran is not a signatory to the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

label: Hezbollah → Northern Israel (Day 19)
damage: Rocket impacts across northern Israel communities
notes: Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets at northern Israel overnight. IDF issued formal evacuation warnings for Tyre area of southern Lebanon. IDF struck Bachoura neighborhood of central Beirut and Bekaa Valley infrastructure. 11 Lebanese Civil Defense members wounded. IDF ground forces pushing deeper into southern Lebanon. US encouraging Syria to assist in disarming Hezbollah.

label: Israel → South Pars Gas Field (Day 20)
damage: Gas field infrastructure struck — operations disrupted
notes: First direct Israeli attack on Iran's upstream gas extraction infrastructure. South Pars is the world's largest natural gas field, shared with Qatar. Strike pushed Brent crude back above $108/bbl. Significant escalation beyond downstream strikes.

label: Israel → Tehran (Intel Minister Khatib)
damage: Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib killed
notes: Targeted assassination of Iran's Intelligence Minister. Adds to the list: Khamenei (Day 1), Larijani (NSC, Mar 17–18), Soleimani-Basij commander, multiple IRGC generals. Iran's intelligence directorate significantly degraded.

label: US B-1 Bombers Over Iranian Airspace (Day 20)
platform: USAF B-1B Lancer / Al Udeid AB
damage: Multiple hardened targets struck in central Iran
notes: US B-1 Lancer bombers observed flying directly over Iranian airspace — a signal of near-total air dominance after Iranian air defenses degraded. Previously B-1s conducted standoff strikes; entering Iranian airspace is a significant escalation. 7,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28.

label: IDF → Tehran (Nowruz Strikes, Day 21)
damage: Infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran
notes: IDF declared 'a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran' on Nowruz. Attacks came despite Day 20 signals of restraint. Tehran residents described hearing multiple explosions across the city. Security forces banned Nowruz gatherings.

label: Iran → Israel (66th Wave — True Promise 4, Super-Heavy)
damage: Targets in heart and south of Israel, including Tel Aviv area
notes: IRGC's 66th wave of Operation True Promise 4. Five super-heavy and multi-warhead missiles deployed. IRGC stated operation executed 'successfully' striking targets in the heart and south of occupied territories and US military bases.

label: Iran → UAE (7 Missiles + 15 Drones Intercepted)
notes: UAE Ministry of Defence confirmed 7 Iranian ballistic missiles and 15 drones intercepted in a Thursday barrage. UAE released video of successful interceptions. No casualties or major infrastructure damage reported.

label: Iran → Qatar Energy Infrastructure (LNG Production Halted)
damage: Qatar forced to halt ALL gas production — global LNG and fertilizer supply disrupted
notes: Iranian counterattacks on energy infrastructure forced Qatar to halt all gas production. Qatar is one of world's biggest LNG producers. Disruption threatens global food security via fertilizer supply chains. UN World Food Programme warns of long-term food price increases. The halt exceeds Kharg Island in immediate global economic impact.

label: USS Tripoli ARG → Middle East (Near Singapore to Arabian Sea)
notes: USS Tripoli ARG (USS Tripoli + USS New Orleans + USS San Diego) with 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) tracked near Singapore March 17, heading to the Middle East. Could support Hormuz mine countermeasures or reinforce Gulf bases. Adds to ~50,000 US troops already in CENTCOM AOR.

label: Iran → Israel (67th Wave — True Promise 4)
damage: Structural damage to residential buildings near Rishon LeZion
notes: IRGC 67th wave of Operation True Promise 4. ~18 ballistic missiles and 24 drones total. 4 missiles reached populated areas in central Israel. IDF reported 6 civilians wounded near Rishon LeZion. No Israeli fatalities. Iran missile launch rate now ~20–25/day, down 93% from Day 1 peak of ~350/day.

label: Iran → Gulf US Bases (67th Wave)
notes: IRGC drone component of 67th wave targeting US military forward bases in the Gulf. Gulf air defense systems — THAAD in Saudi Arabia, UAE Patriot network — intercepted majority. Minor damage to perimeter infrastructure at Al Udeid vicinity reported. No US fatalities.

label: US-Israel → SE Iran (Day 22 — Remaining Launcher Hunt)
damage: Hardened missile launch sites in southeastern Iran struck; IRGC command nodes targeted
notes: Day 22 strikes focused on remaining identified missile infrastructure in southeastern Iran — a region less targeted earlier in the campaign, indicating new intelligence on surviving launcher locations. CENTCOM brought cumulative target count to 7,200+. IDF claims 73% mobile launcher fleet and 87% radar/air defense destroyed. US intelligence assesses Iran's launch capacity will fall below 5–10/day within 7–10 days.

label: IDF → Lebanon (Bekaa Valley + Tyre Area — Day 22)
damage: Bekaa Valley arms depots struck; Tyre-area Hezbollah positions targeted
notes: IDF continued Day 22 strikes on Hezbollah's Bekaa Valley logistics network and Tyre-area defensive positions. Lebanon's death toll rose to 870+. IDF ground forces pressing deeper into southern Lebanon. France-mediated Israel-Lebanon ceasefire track still active but stalled.

label: Hezbollah → Northern Israel (Day 22)
damage: Rocket impacts across northern Israel communities
notes: Hezbollah continued rocket salvos at northern Israeli communities. 13 rockets reached populated areas causing property damage. No reported Israeli fatalities from Hezbollah rockets on Day 22.

label: Iran → Tel Aviv (Mar 24 Missile Strike)
damage: Missile landed in Tel Aviv — structural damage
notes: Iranian missile struck Tel Aviv on Tuesday morning after overnight Israeli airstrikes on Iran. 3 people injured including 1 critically. Concurrent Hezbollah barrage of ~30 rockets struck near Mahanayim Junction in northern Israel — 3 injured. This is part of ongoing IRGC Operation True Promise 4 retaliatory campaign.

label: Iran → Kuwait Airport (Fuel Tank Strike)
damage: Fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport struck, sparking large fire
notes: Iran struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport triggering a fire. No confirmed direct casualties. The strike prompted Saudi Arabia and UAE to reportedly consider joining coalition strikes against Iran (Bloomberg). Kuwait closure/evacuation protocol activated.

label: IDF → Beirut (Bchamoun Residential Strike)
damage: Residential apartment building destroyed in Bchamoun, Beirut suburbs
notes: Israeli airstrike struck residential apartment building in Bchamoun in the Beirut suburbs. Lebanese Ministry of Public Health confirmed 3 killed including a three-year-old girl; 5 wounded. Israel Defense Minister Katz announced Israel would establish a 'security zone' in southern Lebanon stretching to the Litani River (~30km north of border).

label: IDF → Tehran (Cruise Missile + Military Industries)
damage: Military industries, cruise missile production sites, air defense systems, bridges struck
notes: IDF overnight strikes against military industries and cruise missile production facilities in Tehran; weapons production sites, missile manufacturing, and air defense systems targeted. IDF also struck bridges to prevent weapons transfers southward into Lebanon. Financial entities serving Hezbollah targeted. IDF stated ~70% of designated operation targets have been struck.

label: Hezbollah → IDF (South Lebanon — 100+ Rockets)
damage: IDF positions in Naqura, Qawzah and border towns struck
notes: Hezbollah targeted Israeli troops 'massed in the border towns of Naqura and Qawzah' and other border sites with 100+ rockets. IDF continued simultaneous strikes on south Lebanon and Beirut southern suburbs. IDF reported approximately 700 Hezbollah fighters eliminated since start of operations. Lebanon bridge strikes aimed at halting Hezbollah resupply.

label: 82nd Airborne → CENTCOM AOR (1,000+ Troops)
notes: Elements of 82nd Airborne Division HQ, 1st Brigade Combat Team, and enablers (~1,000+ paratroopers) deploying to CENTCOM AOR. Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier and division staff included. Two Marine Expeditionary Units also recently deployed (~4,500 Marines/sailors each). Possible mission includes seizure of Kharg Island (handles 90% of Iranian oil exports). Total US forces in region now ~50,000+.

label: IDF → Kermanshah (Ballistic Missile Sites)
damage: Ballistic missile storage and launch facilities destroyed
notes: IDF struck ballistic missile storage and launch sites in Kermanshah, western Iran. Approx. 70 bombs total dropped across Kermanshah and Dezful in overnight operation. IDF assessed ~75% of designated operation targets hit.

label: IDF → Dezful (Missile Launch Facilities)
damage: Missile launch facilities struck
notes: IDF struck missile launch facilities in Dezful, Khuzestan province (western Iran). Part of the same overnight operation as Kermanshah strikes. IRGC Navy Commander Rear Adm. Alireza Tangsiri — architect of the Hormuz drone blockade — was also killed in the Isfahan area during this operation.

label: Iran → Central Israel (Barrage 1 — Retaliation)
damage: Injuries and damage in central Israel population centers
notes: First of two Iranian retaliatory missile barrages at central Israel following IDF strikes in Kermanshah/Dezful and killing of IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri. Israeli air defenses intercepted the majority. Some damage and injuries reported. Iran firing from pre-stockpiled reserves — production capacity effectively destroyed per IDF/CENTCOM.

label: Iran → Central Israel (Barrage 2 — Follow-On)
damage: Continued damage in central Israel
notes: Second Iranian missile barrage at central Israel on Day 28. Israeli Iron Dome and Arrow systems active. IDF confirmed soldier killed separately from Lebanese rocket fire (not these barrages).

label: Hezbollah → North Israel (IDF Soldier Killed)
notes: Iranian-backed fighters in Lebanon fired rockets into northern Israel. One IDF soldier was killed — bringing Israel's confirmed conflict fatalities to 19+. IDF Chief Zamir warned in cabinet briefing of 'severe strain' with 15,000-soldier manpower shortfall across five simultaneous fronts.

label: Iran → Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia (10 US WIA)
damage: Refueling aircraft damaged; 10 US service members wounded (2 seriously)
notes: First confirmed direct Iranian strike on a major US air base in Saudi Arabia during the conflict. Prince Sultan AB is used for US refueling operations supporting the Iran campaign. CENTCOM confirmed the attack and casualties. Total US wounded now 150+.

label: IDF → Arak Heavy Water Reactor
damage: Heavy water reactor complex struck; plutonium production capability eliminated
notes: Strike targets Iran's Arak IR-40 heavy water research reactor — the country's primary potential source of weapons-grade plutonium. Simultaneously, a yellowcake uranium processing plant in Yazd province was struck. Two major Iranian steel facilities were also hit in the same wave. Iran's nuclear fuel cycle infrastructure now assessed as effectively eliminated.

label: IDF → Yazd Yellowcake Plant
damage: Uranium ore processing plant (yellowcake production) struck
notes: Strike on Yazd province uranium yellowcake production facility eliminates Iran's ability to produce enrichment feedstock from domestic ore. Part of the same overnight nuclear infrastructure strike package as Arak.

label: Iran → Eshtaol (Jerusalem Area) — IDF Intercept Failure
damage: Several lightly injured in Eshtaol; IAF investigating interception failure
notes: Iranian ballistic missile successfully struck Eshtaol, a Jerusalem-area town — IDF failed to intercept. IAF opened investigation. Rocket trails also reported above Netanya. Represents one of Iran's deepest penetrations of Israeli air defenses in the conflict.

label: Houthis → Beersheba, Israel
notes: Houthis resumed missile launches at Israel on March 28 — ballistic missile toward Beersheba triggered air raid sirens in southern Israel. No confirmed casualties. Houthis acting in solidarity with Iranian retaliatory operations.

label: IDF → Beirut Southern Suburbs (Dahiyeh)
damage: Hezbollah infrastructure, media facilities hit; journalists killed
casualties: Lebanese journalists from Al Manar and Al Mayadeen killed
notes: IDF conducted overnight strikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh district (Hezbollah stronghold) and Deir al-Zahrani area in south Lebanon. Al Manar TV and Al Mayadeen journalists killed. Lebanese President Aoun condemned strikes. Lebanon total deaths now 1,142+.

label: IDF → Tehran Residential Areas (Shaft City, Saadat Abad)
damage: 2 killed, 14 wounded in multiple Tehran neighborhoods; power blackouts in Tehran/Karaj
notes: Overnight strikes hit Shaft City, Saadat Abad, and western Tehran residential areas. IRGC ballistic missile R&D complex and air defense sites also targeted. Washington Post investigation confirmed 4 key ballistic missile manufacturing sites and 29+ launch sites damaged across 4 weeks. Power blackouts reported in Tehran and Karaj.

label: IDF → Tabriz Petrochemical Company
damage: Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck; Brent crude surged to ~$115/bbl on news
notes: Strike on the Tabriz Petrochemical Company in northwestern Iran caused Brent crude to spike toward $115/barrel. Part of ongoing campaign targeting Iran's petrochemical and industrial economic infrastructure.

label: Iran → Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Plant (Israel)
damage: Chemical plant hit; hazmat risk; 1 injured
notes: Iranian ballistic missile struck Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone in southern Israel, hitting a chemical plant and raising fears of a hazardous materials leak. Emergency response deployed. Precautionary shelter-in-place orders issued for nearby communities.

label: Iran → ALBA Aluminium Plant (Bahrain)
damage: ALBA aluminum plant struck; 2 employees injured
notes: IRGC targeted ALBA Bahrain aluminum plant, citing alleged US military-aerospace ties. Part of Iran's escalating targeting of Gulf economic and industrial infrastructure beyond oil.

label: Iran → Emirates Global Aluminium (Abu Dhabi)
damage: Significant damage to EGA; 6 workers wounded
notes: IRGC struck Emirates Global Aluminium in Abu Dhabi, one of the world's largest aluminum producers. IRGC cited US military-aerospace ties as justification. Kuwait's National Guard shot down 4 drones in the same wave; UAE air defenses responded to multiple simultaneous threats.

label: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) → Arabian Sea
notes: 3,500 US Marines aboard USS Tripoli arrived in theater. Analysts note deployment is consistent with a Kharg Island seizure mission or Hormuz enforcement operation — both scenarios Trump publicly referenced on March 29.

label: IDF → Iran (continued strikes Day 31)
notes: US-Israeli strike operations continued on Day 31. CENTCOM confirmed Iran's ballistic missile launch rate is down 90% from Day 1; drone launch rates fell 83%. Total Iranian attack profile since Feb 28: 393 attack waves, 414 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,914 UAVs. Iran's missile production capability assessed as effectively destroyed.

label: US → PMF Sites Iraq (Kirkuk, Mosul, Tuz Khurmatu)
damage: PMF sites destroyed; 3 PMF fighters and 2 police officers killed
notes: US airstrikes targeted PMF sites in Mosul's Rashidiya area, Tuz Khurmatu (Salah ad-Din), and Kirkuk International Airport. Iraqi government formally protested as violation of sovereignty.

label: IDF/USAF → Tehran/Isfahan Day 32 (170+ targets)
damage: Drone engine factories, warhead casting plants, missile military HQ destroyed; power outage in Tehran from substation shrapnel
notes: Day 32 wide-scale IDF/USAF strike wave. Targets included drone engine manufacturing, anti-tank missile R&D, warhead casting and filling factories, military HQ in western Iran. Isfahan underground 'missile city,' weapons depots hit. Shiraz and Parchin military complex struck. Hegseth: '200 dynamic strikes overnight.' Trump posted ammo depot strike video (Isfahan). CENTCOM: 11,000+ total targets since Feb 28.

label: Iran → VLCC Al Salmi, Port of Dubai
damage: VLCC Al Salmi struck while fully loaded at Port of Dubai; fire ignited; oil spill risk
notes: Iran struck Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at capacity while anchored at Port of Dubai, igniting a fire and raising fears of a major oil spill. The attack extends Iran's maritime targeting inside a major UAE commercial port. Emergency response deployed. Tanker insurance premiums spiked across the Gulf.

label: IDF → Tehran (Kharazi residence + continued strikes Day 33)
damage: Former FM Kharazi seriously wounded, wife killed; Pakistan diplomatic channel disrupted
notes: IDF strike hit the Tehran home of former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi, seriously wounding him and killing his wife. Kharazi was managing Pakistan back-channel contacts between Iran and US. Day 33 strikes continued across IRGC command nodes and remaining missile storage facilities.

label: Iran → Israel (missile wave — Trump 'destroyed military' response)
damage: Debris falls in populated areas; Iron Dome/Arrow intercept most projectiles
notes: Iran launched a retaliatory missile wave at Israel following Trump's statement claiming the US had 'destroyed the Iranian military.' CENTCOM confirmed the wave. Iran's pre-stockpiled missiles continue to penetrate Israeli air defenses despite missile production being effectively destroyed.

label: Iran → Tanker off Doha, Qatar
damage: Tanker damage reported; no casualties immediately confirmed
notes: British Maritime Trade Operations (BMTO) confirmed a tanker was struck by a projectile off Doha, Qatar. Qatar hosts Al Udeid Air Base — the largest US air base in the region.

label: IDF/USAF → Iran (Day 34 continued strikes)
notes: Day 34 US-Israeli strike operations continued, targeting IRGC command nodes and remaining military infrastructure. Cumulative strikes now exceed 11,000+ targets since February 28. Trump declared the war 'very much complete' while operations continued to force Hormuz reopening before April 6 deadline.

label: IDF → Beirut (Hezbollah commander + Day 34)
damage: Senior Hezbollah commander killed; 7 civilians killed (Lebanese Ministry of Health)
notes: IDF struck Beirut Dahiyeh targeting a senior Hezbollah commander responsible for rocket operations against northern Israel. Lebanese Ministry of Health confirmed 7 civilians killed including women and children.

label: US-Israel → Iran Bridge (Khuzestan)
damage: Bridge struck and destroyed — video-confirmed impact, infrastructure targeting
notes: New video footage confirmed US-Israeli munitions impacted an Iranian bridge. Location approximate based on available intelligence.

label: Houthi Cluster Missile → Ben Gurion Airport
notes: Houthi claim; intercept/damage status unconfirmed. Cluster munitions targeting civilian aviation hub marks significant escalation.

label: Iran → Southern Israel (Industrial Area)
damage: Industrial area struck in southern Israel
notes: Iranian ballistic missile struck an industrial area in southern Israel, Apr 5, 2026. Damage and casualties under assessment.

label: IDF → Lebanon (14+ Killed, Multi-Site)
notes: Israeli strikes across multiple Lebanese locations killed at least 14 people, Apr 5, 2026. Continued multi-front escalation as IDF presses Lebanon campaign simultaneously with Iran strikes.

label: Tehran Synagogue Strike
damage: Civilian synagogue struck inside Tehran
notes: Iran reports US-Israeli projectile struck a synagogue in Tehran. Single source (Al Jazeera/Iranian claim); independent verification pending. Notable escalation targeting civilian religious site in the capital.

label: Strike → Northern Iran
damage: Multiple buildings destroyed in northern Iran
notes: US-Israeli strikes destroy buildings in northern Iran. Video footage documented destruction. Exact targets unconfirmed by CENTCOM/IDF.

label: IDF → South Lebanon (post-ceasefire)
notes: Israeli military strikes southern Lebanon after US-Iran ceasefire announcement. Netanyahu stated ceasefire does not include Lebanon.

label: IDF → Lebanon (fresh strikes, ceasefire threat)
notes: Fresh Israeli airstrikes across Lebanon kill several people on Apr 9, threatening the fragile US-Iran ceasefire framework. Al Jazeera reports continued Israeli operations against Hezbollah despite post-war diplomacy.

label: IDF → Lebanon (healthcare threats, Apr 10)
notes: Israel threatens healthcare infrastructure and continues strikes on Lebanon on Apr 10, straining the fragile US-Iran ceasefire framework.

label: IDF → Lebanese Govt Building (Officers Killed)
notes: Israeli airstrike on a Lebanese government building kills multiple Lebanese military officers on Apr 10. Strike on government infrastructure beyond Hezbollah-specific targets escalates Lebanon front, straining US-Iran ceasefire framework.

label: Hezbollah → Israel (1,500-yr-old Church Damaged)
damage: 1,500-year-old church damaged inside Israel
notes: Hezbollah rocket attack damages a 1,500-year-old church inside Israel on Apr 10. Continued cross-border exchange as Lebanon front remains active alongside ceasefire diplomacy.

label: IDF → Southern Lebanon (18+ Killed, Apr 11)
notes: Israeli bombardment kills at least 18 people across southern Lebanon on Apr 11 — fresh wave distinct from prior Lebanese government building strike. Israel continues Lebanon campaign independently of US-Iran ceasefire framework.
